preview_text:
The model favors Switzerland heavily, assigning them an 81% win probability against a Qatar side given only a 3% chance of victory. The gap reflects a substantial quality differential in the forecast.

The win–draw–loss spread underscores Switzerland's dominance. Draws are plausible at 16%, suggesting the match could remain competitive for stretches, but the model expects a decisive Swiss outcome more often than not. Qatar's low win probability indicates the model sees little path to a three-point result for the underdog.

Model and market are closely aligned. The sportsbook consensus places Switzerland at 79% (versus the model's 81%) and Qatar at 7% (versus 3%), a divergence of a few percentage points on each side. Polymarket prices sit between the two, clustering near the model's estimate. All three sources agree substantially on the match direction, though they differ modestly on the underdog's floor probability.
